title: How IVFFlat lists and probes govern recall
description: IVFFlat partitions vectors into lists at build time; nprobe sets how many lists a query searches, trading recall for speed.
body: Reference for tuning IVFFlat. The index clusters vectors into a fixed number of lists (Voronoi cells) at build time, and each query searches the nprobe lists nearest the query vector, so a low nprobe is fast but can miss neighbours in adjacent cells. Use when configuring or tuning an IVFFlat index. Do not treat the list count as a free knob; too few lists make each list huge and slow, too many under-populate lists and hurt recall, with a common starting point near the square root of the row count. Validation signals include recall@k rising as nprobe increases and a balanced list occupancy. Risk boundary: the list count is fixed at build time, so a poor choice needs a full rebuild, whereas nprobe is a per-query tradeoff you can tune live.
